header.subtitle: Demand range, better usage windows, confidence, and future regional context for the next two days.

errors.context_body: The public dashboard could not load a typed or legacy forecast context.
errors.forecast_body: No forecast points are available for the next 48 hours.
errors.regional_body: The selected future hour has no regional demand allocation.

forecast.title: 48-hour demand outlook
forecast.copy: The selected timestamp from this chart is reused by the explanation, confidence, regional, generation, and balance panels.

regional.title: Regional demand anomaly at {hour}
regional.copy: This is a selected-hour forecast allocation. It is not current regional context and not a regional adequacy status.

viz.title: Actuals, P50 forecast, likely range, and usual-demand baseline
viz.detail: The uncertainty ribbon is the p10-p90 likely range. It is not treated as a positive or negative pressure driver.
viz.source: RTE eco2mix, usual-demand baseline, and typed twin context

controls.objective_caption: These objectives may produce different times because demand balance context and carbon context are separate signals.

best_window.label: Best usage window
best_window.unavailable_detail: No eligible forecast window is available.

delta.at_peak: Selected hour is the expected peak.
delta.after_peak: Selected hour is {hours}h after the expected peak.
delta.before_peak: Selected hour is {hours}h before the expected peak.

terms.fallback_definition: Domain term used by this dashboard.

usual_demand.definition: A comparable-history baseline for the same season, day type, and local hour.

likely_range.definition: The p10 to p90 forecast interval. It describes uncertainty and is not used as a balance-pressure input.

positive_drivers.label: Positive drivers
positive_drivers.detail: Demand-side lift above usual for this selected hour.

negative_drivers.label: Negative drivers
negative_drivers.detail: Demand-side easing below usual for this selected hour.

selected_explanation.title: Deterministic explanation
selected_explanation.label: Numerically reconciled

drivers.lift_detail: The selected forecast sits above the comparable-hour usual-demand baseline.
drivers.easing_detail: The selected forecast sits below the comparable-hour usual-demand baseline.

selected_explanation.text: At {hour}, expected demand is {expected}, {direction}. The deterministic reconciliation is usual demand plus positive drivers minus negative drivers; uncertainty is shown only as the likely range.

weather.unavailable_detail: Weather context is unavailable for this snapshot.

confidence.detail: {level} confidence based on horizon, interval width, route provenance, and recent error evidence.

interval_width.name: Interval width
interval_width.detail: Width of the p10-p90 likely range relative to expected demand.

weather_disagreement.name: Weather disagreement
weather_disagreement.detail: No multi-provider weather-disagreement artifact is attached to this route.

recent_performance.name: Recent model performance
recent_performance.detail: Most recent route-level error metric available to the page.

regional.unavailable_detail: No selected-hour regional forecast was returned by the typed twin.
regional.typed_note: Selected-hour regional demand allocation from the typed twin.
regional.projected_method: Current regional demand shares projected onto the selected national forecast hour.
regional.projected_note: Projected regional demand is context only; it is not a current reading and not a regional adequacy status.

mix.label: Estimated generation mix
mix.model_detail: Nuclear, wind, solar, and residual flexible sources/imports for the selected future hour.
mix.fallback_detail: Latest generation mix carried as an explicit fallback assumption for the selected future demand.

availability.label: Availability context
availability.fallback_detail: No selected-hour unavailability feed is aligned; latest availability is fallback context only.

official.label: Official signal
official.unavailable_detail: No aligned official signal is shown for this selected future hour.

carbon.label: Carbon context
carbon.model_detail: Selected-hour carbon intensity estimate for context; it is not a balance input.
carbon.fallback_detail: Latest carbon intensity carried as fallback context; it is not a balance input.

exchange.label: Exchange estimate
exchange.model_detail: Positive means net imports; negative means net exports.
exchange.fallback_detail: Latest exchange context carried as a fallback assumption.

how_calculated.label: How was this calculated?
how_calculated.paragraph_1: The page uses the typed electricity-system twin only when its forecast origin aligns with the page's latest actual or demo timestamp. Otherwise it uses the aligned 48-hour forecast and labels projected regional, generation, balance, and carbon context as fallback.
how_calculated.paragraph_2: Demand is shown as p50 with a p10-p90 likely range and a usual-demand baseline. The selected-hour explanation reconciles p50 as usual demand plus positive drivers minus negative drivers.
how_calculated.paragraph_3: The best-window selector evaluates rolling three-hour windows. Balance, carbon, and combined objectives can choose different times because they optimize different quantities.

data_sources.label: Data sources and freshness
data_sources.unaligned_twin: Typed twin snapshots were available but not aligned to this page's latest forecast origin, so selected-hour context is shown with explicit fallback labels.

about.label: About the project
about.paragraph_1: This hackathon decision-support prototype is built for public electricity awareness. It combines RTE/ODRE electricity observations, EcoWatt context, weather features, and transparent fallbacks to explain demand, supply, and pressure separately.
about.paragraph_2: Limitations: this is not an RTE operational forecast, tariff signal, dispatch tool, or verified carbon accounting method. Historical sample data is separated from live operational context.

generation.fallback_formula: Latest observed/replay mix carried as fallback context.

explanations.lowest_balance: Lowest average balance score over the window.
explanations.lowest_carbon: Lowest average carbon-intensity context over the window.
explanations.combined: Combined score balancing app balance context, carbon, and demand.
